scope_note: 2026 model-year update. Only bikes with 2026 changes or verified 2026 availability (year extensions) are included; the seeder rebuilds each included bike wholesale. Excluded as no-change/discontinued: segway-x260, segway-x160 (line ended ~2023; replaced by the new Xaber platform), super73-rx, super73-r-brooklyn (ended pre-MY24), super73-s2, super73-z-miami (no verified 2026 model year; the March-2026 A-Series is Super73's new lineup). Prior-era rows keep v2 catalog_name strings EXACTLY for DB merge; 2026 rows are year-qualified.

bikes.motor: Mid-mounted PMSM: 5kW peak (2018-2021), 6kW peak (2022-2025); 10kW peak 72V PMSM, 295Nm (2026+)
bikes.year_notes: US market entry May 2018, not 2019 as v1 stated. Year log per Electric Cycle Rider '5 Years With The Surron Light Bee X' (2024, corroborated by Ronhack): 2019 sealed chain/bearings/footpegs; 2020 cable throttle replaced by electronic throttle, OEM chain box; 2021 dual-hall throttle, thicker brake discs, KKE front/rear suspension adopted (mixed with DNM by production availability), Gates primary belt, battery to 34Ah; 2022 power 5kW to 6kW, battery 38.5Ah, new stem/front fender; 2023 battery 40Ah. Factory Minibikes tech page adds a pre/post-May-2019 speed-limiter wiring difference (green/black wire loop behavior flips). US-market bikes were still commonly listed with 32Ah packs into 2021-2022, so verify the pack label at intake, not the model year. NEW FOR 2026 - the biggest platform change in the bike's history, verified against two independent sources (surron.ae '2026 Light Bee X vs 2025' comparison; ATV Wholesale Outlet US dealer spec page): voltage platform moves 60V to 72V with a 35Ah (2.5kWh) pack and 10-second quick-release; peak power to 10kW / 295Nm; new Smart FOC controller (balanced three-phase output, adaptive throttle algorithm, 3-level throttle response, S/E modes); new KKE 200mm-travel fork with adjustable preload and rebound; all-new 'TR Intersect' link-type multi-link rear suspension with 267mm adjustable shock and 450lb spring; upgraded 4-piston DOT4 hydraulic brakes (203mm F/R per the US dealer page). US dealer pricing $4,499-4,999. CRITICAL INTAKE RULE: 2026 parts do NOT pool with any prior-era Light Bee part - different voltage platform, different controller family, different rear linkage. Dealers are still selling 2025-spec 60V bikes as new alongside 2026 stock, so identify the donor by pack voltage label and rear linkage, never by sale date. 19in wheel/tire spec (70/100-19) carries into 2026 unchanged, so the shared tire pool still applies.
bikes.blurb: The default halo e-moto. Every owner strips it in month one, so takeoff supply is bottomless and resale is slow. The 2026 72V overhaul resets the parts platform - treat 2026 takeoffs as a new family.

stock_parts.comp_note: Stock rear shock on 2018-2020 Light Bees (ECR year log: KKE rear adopted 2021, though DNM still appears in some later runs). Same physical part as the Segway X260 shock so the pool merges; catalog_name kept unchanged. New RCP-2S retails $165 at Luna. Takeoff pulls saturate r/Surron and eBay and sit unsold; no confirmed sold prices visible, so pegged well under half of new.

upgrade.name: Fastace BDA53RC rear shock
upgrade.note: First thing everyone bins. The stock DNM is the fuse in the system, this one actually damps.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Light Bee KKE stock rear shock (2021-2025)
stock_parts.comp_note: 2021+ production adopted KKE rear suspension per ECR year log, but supply mixed with DNM by production run, so identify the unit on the shock body at intake, not by year alone. Era ends at 2025: the 2026 bike moves to the TR Intersect multi-link and a different shock. Same upgrade culture as the DNM era; no sold comps found, pegged low.

upgrade.name: Fastace BDA53RC rear shock
upgrade.note: Same swap regardless of which OEM shock the bike shipped with.

stock_parts.stock_part: TR Intersect multi-link rear shock, 267mm adjustable with 450lb spring
stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Light Bee TR multi-link rear shock (2026+)
stock_parts.comp_note: New-for-2026 link-type rear suspension (surron.ae comparison: 'Intersect TR multi-link', 267mm adjustable shock, 450lb high-strength spring; ATV Wholesale lists it as a KKE-built TR Intersect link-type unit). Different mounting from every prior LBX shock - does NOT pool with DNM or 2021-2025 KKE rows. Nearly-new takeoffs will appear as the mod crowd re-springs; supply will flood once aftermarket fitments land, so pegged conservative with zero sold history.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Light Bee stock inverted fork (DNM takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: The DNM USD-8 is the 2018-2020 stock fork (ECR: KKE adopted 2021). Fork upgrades (Fastace, KKE) are the most common LBX mod, so stock fork takeoffs are everywhere. Retail replacements run $300 plus but used pulls move slow; pegged low. Catalog name kept from v1 since it already identifies the DNM part.

upgrade.name: Fastace ALX13RC 2.0 fork
upgrade.note: The standard fork swap. Stock DNM legs flex and blow seals when you jump the thing.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Light Bee KKE stock fork (2021-2025)
stock_parts.comp_note: KKE became an OEM fork supplier from 2021 (ECR year log; Sur-Ron's own store sells the 'Light Bee KKE Front Fork'; elecycles cross-lists KKE/DNM stock forks). Production mixed KKE and DNM by availability, so verify the brand stamped on the lowers at intake. Era ends at 2025: the 2026 bike ships a revised 200mm adjustable KKE fork listed as its own row. Slightly stronger resale than the DNM era fork; still no confirmed solds, pegged low.

upgrade.name: Fastace ALX13RC 2.0 fork
upgrade.note: Jumpers still swap the KKE-era fork; better than the old DNM but not by enough.

stock_parts.stock_part: KKE 200mm-travel inverted fork, adjustable preload and rebound
stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Light Bee KKE stock fork (2026+)
stock_parts.comp_note: 2026 fork per ATV Wholesale US spec page: KKE telescopic inverted unit, 200mm travel, adjustable preload and rebound - a real step over the 2021-2025 non-adjustable KKE. Kept as a separate year-qualified pool: the adjusters make it visually identifiable at intake. Nearly-new pulls will command more than old-era forks at first, but 2026 supply will flood as the Fastace crowd swaps anyway; pegged conservative, no sold history.

upgrade.name: Fastace ALX13RC 2.0 fork
upgrade.note: Serious jumpers still step up even from the adjustable 2026 unit.

stock_parts.comp_note: 5kW-era controller, cross-compatible with Segway X260 per eBay/AIC Motorsports listings, so the pools merge and the shared catalog_name is kept. Note the pre/post-May-2019 limiter wiring difference (Factory Minibikes): wire-loop behavior flips, worth flagging in the listing. Asks cluster $100-200; sold prices assumed lower on saturated supply.

upgrade.name: EBMX X-9000 V3 controller kit
upgrade.note: The big power play. Stock controller comes off the day the EBMX box lands.

stock_parts.comp_note: Power rating rose from 5kW to 6kW with the 2022 model year (ECR year log), which is a controller/motor re-rate, so 2022+ controllers are split from the earlier pool. Era ends at 2025: the 2026 bike runs a 72V Smart FOC unit, listed separately. Connector revisions across runs are reported in the community; photograph plugs at intake. No confirmed solds; pegged low against the same saturated-supply dynamics.

upgrade.name: EBMX X-9000 V3 controller kit
upgrade.note: Same story in the 6kW era. Stock unit lands in a drawer.

stock_parts.comp_note: New-for-2026 Smart FOC controller on the 72V platform (surron.ae: balanced three-phase output, adaptive rider-input algorithm; ATV Wholesale: FOC sine wave, 10kW system). NOT interchangeable with any 60V-era controller - different voltage, different pool. Aftermarket controller kits for the 72V platform are unverified as of this writing, so swap pressure starts lower than the 60V era; expect it to rise once EBMX-class kits ship. Zero sold history; pegged conservative.

No evidence of a tire spec change across the production run, and the 2026 spec page still lists 70/100-19 front and rear, so this pool now covers 2018-2026. Street conversions pull them with near-zero wear. Cheap new (about $35 each), so used pairs fetch little. No sold comps found, pegged low.

upgrade.name: Shinko 241 street tire pair
upgrade.note: The go-to street conversion tire. Stock knobbies squirm on pavement.

stock_parts.comp_note: The classic 32Ah pack (176 Panasonic cells per Factory Minibikes). Nominally 2018-2020, but US-market bikes commonly shipped 32Ah into 2021-2022, so ALWAYS price off the pack label, not the frame year. New OEM packs run $800 eBay to $1,599 dealer; used guidance is 40-60 percent under retail. Cycle count unverifiable at intake, pegged conservative.

upgrade.name: Amorge 72V 35Ah conversion battery
upgrade.note: The 72V conversion crowd pulls the stock pack while it still holds a charge.

stock_parts.comp_note: ECR year log: capacity stepped to 34Ah in 2021 then 38.5Ah in 2022, with market/region overlap (transitional 34Ah packs exist and some US 2021-22 units still carried 32Ah). Verify the printed Ah on the pack at intake. Higher capacity holds a modest premium over the 32Ah pool; no confirmed solds, pegged conservative.

upgrade.name: Amorge 72V 35Ah conversion battery
upgrade.note: Same 72V conversion path frees these packs up healthy.

stock_parts.comp_note: Third capacity bump per ECR year log; 40Ah is the pack on 2023-2025 Light Bee X retail pages. Era ends at 2025 - the 2026 platform is 72V and its pack is a separate row. Newest and most desirable 60V pack, still discounts hard used on unverifiable health. No confirmed solds; base pegged at 40 percent of a conservative comp.

upgrade.name: Amorge 72V 35Ah conversion battery
upgrade.note: Even 40Ah owners chasing 72V power pull these while fresh.

stock_parts.comp_note: The 2026 platform pack: 72V 35Ah, 2.5kWh, with a 10-second quick-release mount (surron.ae; ATV Wholesale). Factory 72V finally kills the Amorge conversion play on this generation, so packs come off less often - expect crash-part and range-upgrade supply only. NOT interchangeable with any 60V bike or with aftermarket 72V conversion packs (different mount). Zero resale history and 2026 takeoffs are nearly new, but supply will flood as bikes churn; pegged conservative at 40 percent of a cautious comp.

stock_parts.stock_part: Upgraded 4-piston hydraulic brake set, DOT 4, 203mm rotors
stock_parts.comp_note: 2026 brake upgrade verified by both sources (surron.ae: forged 4-piston system, larger pistons, thicker lines; ATV Wholesale: DOT 4 hydraulic, 203mm F/R). First LBX generation where brakes are a listable takeoff - prior-era 2-piston pulls were giveaway items with no row. Swap pressure is low (the new brakes are good), so supply comes from crash parts; no sold history, pegged low.

No evidence of a seat change across years, 2026 included. Tall and comfort seat swaps are common; stock plank seats pile up. No sold comps found, low ask assumed. If 2026 bodywork proves incompatible at intake, split the pool then.

upgrade.name: Charged Cycle Works tall comfort seat
upgrade.note: Stock seat is a 2x4 with vinyl on it. Taller riders swap it week one.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron 48T stock rear sprocket and chain (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: 48T/420 spec constant across the run (Factory Minibikes). 2019 added a sealed chain but same spec, so no split. No verified drivetrain change on the 2026 spec pages. Wear items discount hard used; pegged low, no sold comps found.

upgrade.name: GritShift 54T aluminum rear sprocket
upgrade.note: Torque gearing for trail riders. Steel stocker comes off half worn.

stock_parts.comp_note: Pegs were 'improved' in 2019 per ECR but remained small cast units the community still bins, so one pool. No verified 2026 peg change. Nearly giveaway items in group sales; no individual sold comps found.

upgrade.name: Warp 9 billet footpegs
upgrade.note: Stock pegs are small and slick. Billet pegs are the cheapest confidence upgrade on the bike.

bikes.motor: Mid-mounted PMSM: 12.5kW peak (2023-2024); V2/HP hairpin-wound motor 18kW sustained, 21kW peak (2025-2026)
bikes.year_notes: Two real generations plus one mid-year change. Launch spec (2023): 12.5kW peak, 74V 55Ah, 19in wheels both ends. Mid-2024: rear wheel switched from 19in to 18in during the model year (Cycle News 2025 Ultra Bee HP review), so 2024 bikes exist both ways. 2025 V2/HP: hairpin-wound motor at 18kW sustained/21kW peak with Turbo and Crawl modes, 74V 60Ah pack, upgraded suspension and brakes (Cycle News; GritShift 'Ultra Bee V2 Tested By Pro Riders'). V2 parts do not merge with 2023-2024 pools. 2026 UPDATE: the Ultra Bee HP carries into 2026 unchanged - US dealers list 'Ultra Bee HP 2026' at the same 21kW/18kW, 74V 60Ah, forged double-cradle frame, six-ride-mode spec (sur-ron.us.com, Creature Racing, surronpart.com). No new component changes verified for 2026, so V2/HP rows are extended to 2025-2026 with catalog names unchanged.
bikes.blurb: The grown-up Sur-Ron. Better stock parts than the Light Bee but owners still chase Sirris suspension and EBMX power, so takeoffs flow steadily.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Ultra Bee stock rear shock (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Gen-1 (2023-2024) shock; 2025+ V2 got revised suspension so pools are split. Sirris R46 is the recommended swap per Electric Cycle Rider and Darwin EV, so stock shocks come off constantly. No sold comps visible; pegged low against thick supply.

upgrade.name: Sirris R46 rear shock
upgrade.note: Purpose-tuned for the Ultra Bee. Stock shock fades once you ride it like the ads show.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock Ultra Bee V2 rear shock (revised 2025 spec)
stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Ultra Bee V2 rear shock (2025)
stock_parts.comp_note: V2/HP suspension was 'greatly improved' over gen-1 per Cycle News, so this is a distinct part with fewer reasons to come off. Spec carries into the 2026 Ultra Bee HP unchanged, so the pool now covers 2025-2026 (catalog name kept for DB merge). Model still too new for any resale evidence; floor value only.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock Ultra Bee inverted fork (9.4in travel)
stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Ultra Bee stock inverted fork (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Gen-1 fork; 2025 V2 suspension revision splits the pool. Stock fork is decent so swap rate is lower than the Light Bee, but Sirris F43 and KKE 37mm conversions still generate takeoffs. No sold comps found.

upgrade.name: Sirris F43 fork
upgrade.note: More travel and real damping for riders who actually jump the bike.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock Ultra Bee V2 inverted fork (revised 2025 spec, 50% stiffer)
stock_parts.comp_note: Upgraded for 2025 per Cycle News (dealer pages cite a 50% stiffer fork on the HP), so fewer swaps expected and no takeoff market exists yet. Carries into 2026 unchanged; pool extended, catalog name kept for DB merge. Floor value only.

Gen-1 12.5kW controller. The 2025+ V2/HP runs a different controller (18kW sustained, Turbo/Crawl modes) and does not merge. One pre-owned eBay listing at $299 ask, not a confirmed sale; pegged well under the ask.

upgrade.name: EBMX X-9000 V3 controller kit
upgrade.note: Same story as every Sur-Ron. Stock FOC unit is fine until the owner wants 50kW.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock V2/HP controller (18kW sustained, Turbo/Crawl modes)
stock_parts.comp_note: New 2025 unit powering the hairpin motor (Cycle News, GritShift V2 review); unchanged on the 2026 Ultra Bee HP, so the pool extends to 2025-2026 (catalog name kept for DB merge). Strong enough that swap pressure is lower; no resale market exists yet. Floor value only.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock 19in front and rear moto knobby pair
stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Ultra Bee 19in knobby tire pair (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: 19/19 wheel setup applies to 2023 through mid-2024 only: Sur-Ron switched the rear to 18in DURING the 2024 model year (Cycle News), so check the rear rim stamp on any 2024 bike. Supermoto and MX tire swaps are routine; used OEM knobbies move cheap, no sold comps found.

upgrade.name: Shinko 505 Cheater tire set
upgrade.note: The trail-bite upgrade. Stock rubber is a compromise compound.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock 19in front / 18in rear moto knobby set
stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Ultra Bee 19F/18R knobby tire set (2024-2025)
stock_parts.comp_note: Late-2024 onward runs the 18in rear for better tire selection and off-road performance (Cycle News); spec continues on the 2026 HP, so the pool extends to 2024-2026 (catalog name kept for DB merge). Different rear size means these do NOT pool with the 19/19 pairs. Same cheap-used dynamics; no sold comps found.

upgrade.name: Shinko 505 Cheater tire set
upgrade.note: 18in rear opens up real moto rubber choices; stock set still comes off.

stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of a seat change across 2023-2026 (V2 chassis 'largely unchanged' per Cycle News; 2026 HP continues it). Tall seat swaps common for adult riders. No sold comps found, estimate kept low.

upgrade.name: Charged Cycle Works tall comfort seat
upgrade.note: Adds standover comfort for full-size riders on long trail days.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Ultra Bee stock rear sprocket and chain (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of a drivetrain spec change through 2026. Wear item with cheap new replacements. No sold comps found; pegged low.

upgrade.name: Warp 9 aluminum rear sprocket
upgrade.note: Lighter 7075 aluminum and gearing choice in one swap.

stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of a peg change across years, 2026 included. Billet peg swaps near universal on ridden bikes. No individual sold comps found.

upgrade.name: GritShift billet footpegs
upgrade.note: Wider platform, actual grip. Stock pegs go in the parts bin.

bikes.year_notes: Corrections carried from v2: v1 listed '96V 48Ah' and years 2021-2025. The 96V figure came from pre-production press; delivered bikes run a 90V-nominal 48Ah pack, 4.3kWh (Electric Cycle Rider's Sept 2022 range test of a production bike; Flash E-Bikes sells the OEM pack as 'Storm Bee 90V 48Ah Sony battery'). Bike was unveiled in 2020 but meaningful deliveries started 2022. Some sources (Mudbike) claim a later '104V 55Ah' production pack; unverified in the wild and possibly a max-charge-voltage/nominal confusion, so no year split; verify the pack label on any intake. 2026 UPDATE: still in production and sold - US dealers list a 2026 model (SKU SSB2026 at surronmotors.com; sur-ronusa.us.com and us.surron.com carry it; April 2026 customer reviews confirm active sales) in MX, enduro, and road-legal configurations. No verified component changes for 2026, so years extend to 2022-2026 with all rows unchanged. US units remain grey-market and thin.
bikes.blurb: Full-size 90V flagship, still in production but thin on the ground in the US. Takeoff parts exist in small numbers and resale evidence is nearly nonexistent.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Storm Bee stock rear shock (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of year-to-year change through 2026. Very few US bikes so almost no takeoff sales found. Pegged low on thin evidence and a tiny buyer pool.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Storm Bee stock inverted fork (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of year-to-year change through 2026. Full-size fork with moto-crossover value but no comps found for used Storm Bee pulls. Conservative estimate for a tiny market.
System is 90V nominal (v1's '96V' label kept in catalog_name for DB merge; description corrected). No resale sales found anywhere indexed. No realistic exit price, so floor value only.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Storm Bee 21/18 knobby tire pair (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of change. Full-size moto tire sizes at least have a broad dirtbike buyer pool. Still no Storm Bee specific comps found; used knobby pairs are cheap everywhere.

upgrade.name: Shinko 546 front and 505 rear tire set
upgrade.note: Standard full-size dirtbike rubber. Stock tires come off for terrain-specific sets.

stock_parts.comp_note: No used market found. Floor value only.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Storm Bee stock rear sprocket and chain (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Wear item, no used market found. Floor value only.

bikes.motor: 8kW peak mid-mounted motor, 185Nm max wheel torque, vector sine-wave controller
bikes.year_notes: NEW MODEL, verified: launched September 2025 as Sur-Ron's 2026 minibike-class entry (surron.ca launch blog 'Introducing the 2026 Surron Hyper Bee'; us.surron.com/hyperbee; Ronhack and multiple US dealers list it as '2026 Hyper Bee'). Verified specs (Ronhack 2026 product page, corroborated by dealer listings): 8kW peak, 185Nm wheel torque, 40mph top speed, 58V 22Ah quick-release ternary pack with portable 50.4V 5A magnetic charger, vector sine-wave controller, 6.7in fork travel, 2.4in shock travel (7.3in wheel travel), 14in front (60/100-14) and 12in rear (70/100-12) off-road tires, forged aluminum frame, about 86lb, 26.9in adjustable seat height, three ride modes plus reverse, SAWC anti-wheelie control, $2,899 US. Slots below the Light Bee X as the kid/pit-bike-size Sur-Ron - closest competitor to the Talaria X3 and E-Ride Pro-SS class. Model is months old: assume near-zero takeoff resale evidence and price everything at floors until real solds appear.
bikes.blurb: Sur-Ron's new-for-2026 minibike. Nearly-new takeoffs will trickle out as the mod crowd gets hold of it, but there is no resale history yet - buy at floors only.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Hyper Bee stock inverted fork (2026+)
stock_parts.comp_note: Travel spec verified (6.7in per Ronhack); fork supplier/brand not published, so identify markings at intake. Sur-Ron minibike fork swaps will follow the Light Bee pattern once aftermarket support lands, but no resale market exists yet. Floor value only.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock rear shock (2.4in stroke, 7.3in wheel travel)
stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Hyper Bee stock rear shock (2026+)
stock_parts.comp_note: Stroke/travel verified (Ronhack); shock supplier not published. No resale market yet; floor value only.

Vector sine-wave unit with three ride modes, reverse, 3-level power curve and SAWC anti-wheelie (Ronhack spec sheet). No aftermarket controller kits verified for this platform yet, and no resale market. Floor value only.

58V 22Ah hot-swap pack verified (Ronhack; dealer pages). Hot-swap design means owners buy SECOND packs rather than pulling the first, so takeoff supply will be crash bikes only. No pricing history anywhere; floor value until solds exist.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Sur-Ron Hyper Bee 14/12 knobby tire pair (2026+)
stock_parts.comp_note: Sizes verified (Ronhack). 14/12 is the standard 110cc pit-bike tire size with a real existing buyer pool, so this is the one Hyper Bee part with immediate cross-model demand. Cheap new equivalents cap it; pegged low.
No used market exists. Floor value only.

bikes.motor: 8kW peak mid-mounted IPM (2023-2025); Evo/Expert refresh adds transfer-case gearbox, Expert claims +10% peak power (2026)
bikes.year_notes: Years and specs verified: MX4 (sold as 'Sting R' at launch) released 2023 with 8kW IPM motor, 60V 45Ah pack, 220mm rotors (GritShift MX4 deep dive; pitbikeusa comparisons). CRITICAL intake distinction: the 2022 Sting MX3 is a DIFFERENT bike (6kW PMSM, 60V 38.4Ah, 203mm rotors) sharing the frame but with non-interchangeable motor and battery and different headlight/kill-switch connectors (GritShift, Talaria Canada) - MX3 parts must not be listed against this entry. The MX5 (72V 40Ah, 13.4kW) is a separate model with its own entry in v3. 2026 UPDATE: the Sting R MX4 line was refreshed into 'Evo' and 'Expert' editions, live on Talaria US/Canada dealer pages in the 2026 cycle (talariacanada.com, talaria.us.com, talariastingr.us.com). Shared Evo/Expert updates per dealer pages: transfer case (gearbox), wider rear wheel, real-time adjustable motor regen, improved battery connector, stronger headset bearings, and larger/thicker brake rotors quoted as 220x2.3mm vs the previous 203x2.0mm (NOTE: earlier GritShift material claimed 220mm at launch, so rotor history is contested - measure at intake). Expert edition adds Manitou Dorado Expert 37mm USD forks and a claimed 10% peak-power bump; Evo is the same bike with the Talaria factory fork. The 60V 45Ah pack carries over, and base-spec MX4s continue alongside, so existing null-year pools remain valid - identify Evo/Expert donors by the gearbox and (Expert) Manitou fork at intake.
bikes.blurb: Sting MX4 and Sting R MX4 are the same bike per GritShift and Talaria dealers, so one entry covers both names. Sur-Ron's main rival with the same strip-it-day-one owner culture. The 2026 Evo/Expert refresh adds a gearbox and, on Expert, a genuinely valuable Manitou fork.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Talaria Sting MX4 factory rear shock (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Suspension upgrades are the top Talaria mod category at every retailer, so stock shocks flood out. No confirmed sold prices found; pegged low. MX3 suspension components physically similar but keep pools separate on connector-era caution. No verified shock change on the 2026 Evo/Expert, so the pool stays year-open.

upgrade.name: Fastace BDA53RC rear shock
upgrade.note: Same swap as the Sur-Ron crowd makes. Stock shock is serviceable, this is better everywhere.

stock_parts.comp_note: New factory fork 2.0 retails $699 at Luna but used takeoffs are plentiful and slow movers. No sold comps found; pegged far under new. Pool stays year-open: the 2026 Evo edition still ships the Talaria factory fork (only Expert gets the Manitou, listed as its own row).

upgrade.name: KKE 37mm front fork kit
upgrade.note: Stiffer legs for riders who case jumps. The factory fork is fine right up until it is not.

stock_parts.stock_part: Manitou Dorado Expert 37mm USD fork (Expert edition)
stock_parts.catalog_name: Talaria Sting MX4 Manitou Dorado Expert fork (2026+)
stock_parts.comp_note: The 2026 Sting R MX4 Expert ships Manitou Dorado Expert USD forks - a redesigned 37mm-chassis version of a legitimately premium MTB fork (talaria.us.com Expert Edition page; talaria-ebikes.us). This is the rare STOCK part worth real money: Dorado Experts retail near four figures in the bicycle world. Swap pressure is low (nobody bins a Dorado), so supply is crash parts only. Zero e-moto resale history and unknown crown/steerer compatibility with MTB buyers, so pegged far below bicycle-market pricing.

MX4-specific 8kW controller; NOT interchangeable with MX3 (different motor tech and connectors per GritShift). EBMX sells a plug-and-play MX4 kit so pulls are common. No sold comps visible; pegged low against saturated supply. CAUTION on 2026 Evo/Expert donors: the Expert claims a 10% power bump and the refresh changed the battery connector - photograph plugs and the controller label at intake before pooling.

upgrade.name: EBMX X-9000 V3 controller kit
upgrade.note: Plug-and-play on the MX4. Stock controller comes off working and lands in a drawer.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Talaria Sting 19in CST knobby tire pair (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Same saturation story as Sur-Ron. Cheap new, cheaper used, street converters pull them nearly unworn. Note the 2026 Evo/Expert runs a wider rear wheel - rear tire spec may differ on refresh donors; verify sizes at intake before pooling.

upgrade.name: Shinko 241 street tire pair
upgrade.note: Supermoto conversion staple for pavement-only Stings.

stock_parts.comp_note: 45Ah is MX4-only; the MX3's 38.4Ah pack is a different, non-interchangeable part (GritShift) - check the label. New spare packs run about $1,500 at Luna. Used pack pricing tracks 50 to 60 percent under new with unverifiable cycle counts; pegged conservative. The 45Ah pack carries over on 2026 Evo/Expert but with an IMPROVED CONNECTOR per dealer pages - photograph the connector at intake and flag refresh-era packs in the listing.

upgrade.name: eWatt 60V 53Ah battery
upgrade.note: Range chasers step up capacity and sell the stock pack while it still tests healthy.

stock_parts.comp_note: Tall seat swaps common. No sold comps found; low estimate. Seat/plastics shared across Sting generations per GritShift, so this pool can absorb MX3 seats.

upgrade.name: Charged Cycle Works tall comfort seat
upgrade.note: Stock foam packs out fast. Taller riders swap early.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Talaria Sting stock rear sprocket and chain (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Wear item with cheap new replacements. Pegged low, no sold comps found. 2026 Evo/Expert adds a transfer-case gearbox - final-drive parts on refresh donors may differ; verify before pooling.

upgrade.name: GritShift aluminum rear sprocket
upgrade.note: Gearing swap for trail torque. Stock steel ring is heavy.

stock_parts.comp_note: Universal billet peg swap culture. Stock pegs are giveaway items in bundle sales. Shared across Sting generations.

upgrade.name: GritShift billet footpegs
upgrade.note: Cheap, grippy, and the stock pegs deserve the bin.

bikes.year_notes: NEW ENTRY (previously only name-checked in the MX4 dossier notes). Launched late 2024, so US model years run 2025-2026 (GritShift 'Talaria Sting MX5 Tested By Pro Riders'; Talaria USA/Canada and Luna Cycle product pages). Verified specs: 13.4kW peak IPM motor, 500Nm rear-wheel torque, 55+ mph, 72V 40Ah pack (2,880Wh, Samsung 50S 21700 cells) - the biggest pack in the Talaria range and a 45% power step over the 60V bikes; 220x3.0mm oversized rotors with larger calipers and DOT master cylinders; adjustable air + dual-spring suspension; three ride modes (Eco/Sport/Hyper) plus 4-level regen; color TFT display; 165lb net. GritShift's pro-rider verdict was lukewarm ('not the best 72V around'), which matters for resale: expect owners to mod suspension early. NOTHING on this bike pools with 60V Sting parts - different voltage platform, different brakes. No verified 2025-to-2026 running changes, so no year splits within the entry.
bikes.blurb: Talaria's 72V flagship, pitched at the Ultra Bee. Young parts market with almost no resale history - price at floors and let the first real solds teach us.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock MX5 adjustable dual-spring rear shock
stock_parts.catalog_name: Talaria Sting MX5 stock rear shock (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Adjustable dual-spring unit per Talaria dealer spec pages. GritShift's review flagged suspension as the weak point, so swap pressure should be real once aftermarket fitments land. No resale market yet; floor value only.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Talaria Sting MX5 stock air fork (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Adjustable air fork per dealer spec pages; supplier not published, so photograph fork markings at intake. Same early-swap expectation as the shock. No resale market yet; floor value only.
72V unit with Eco/Sport/Hyper modes and 4-level regen. NOT interchangeable with any 60V Talaria controller. No aftermarket kit or resale market verified yet; floor value only.

The biggest, newest pack in the Talaria line (verified: Samsung 50S cells, 2,880Wh - Talaria USA/Luna spec pages). Replacement packs will retail well north of $1,500 by analogy to the 60V 45Ah at $1,500, but there are ZERO used solds and cycle health is unverifiable, so the comp is a deliberate lowball and base follows at 40 percent. Never pay more than a takeoff pack can actually exit at.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Talaria Sting MX5 knobby tire pair (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: Stock knobby fitment; published size claims vary across dealer pages, so record actual sizes at intake before pooling with any other tire row. Standard strip-and-street culture applies. No MX5-specific comps; pegged low.

upgrade.name: Shinko 505 Cheater tire set
upgrade.note: Same terrain-rubber swap the Ultra Bee crowd makes.

stock_parts.comp_note: Color TFT dash verified on dealer spec pages. Spare-part demand only (crash replacements). No resale market; floor value.
No used market exists yet. Floor value only.

bikes.motor: 6kW peak mid-mounted IPM with belt primary
bikes.year_notes: Launch dating is muddy: US dealer pages date the 'X3 Concept' to Feb 2025, while US market chatter has bikes circulating from 2024; keeping 2024 start with low confidence. Verified specs: 6kW peak IPM, 60V 40Ah LG 21700 M50 pack, 17in supermoto wheels (70/90-17 front on 1.6in rim, 90/80-17 rear on 2.15in rim), 4-piston hydraulic brakes with 220mm rotors, belt primary (Talaria dealer spec pages). 2026 UPDATE: the X3/XXX remains in Talaria's current US lineup (talariaebikebrand.com lists the full 2026 range including X3 and XXX; talaria.us.com sells both), so years extend to 2026 with no verified changes to the BASE bike. The higher-spec 'X3 Pro' got a 2025 update (UL certification, color TFT replacing the OLED, triple-LED headlight, battery to 40Ah max per talaria.us.com/talaria-ebikes.us Pro pages) - the Pro remains a SEPARATE variant: treat Pro parts separately at intake, do not merge, especially displays and headlights.
bikes.blurb: Talaria's 17in supermoto pit bike, sold as X3 or xXx. Newer and lower volume than the Sting, so the used parts market is thin and prices must stay careful.

stock_parts.comp_note: Model is new and low volume; no used comps found. Priced off Sting shock behavior with a further discount for the smaller buyer pool.
No used comps found for X3 forks. Conservative estimate against a thin market.
No resale market found for X3 controllers yet. Floor value only.
Sizes verified against dealer spec pages (70/90-17 front, 90/80-17 rear). 17in moto sizes have some crossover demand but no X3 specific comps found. Pegged low.

upgrade.note: Real motorcycle rubber for a bike that gets ridden like a motorcycle.

stock_parts.comp_note: No used market found. Floor value only.
No used market found. Floor value only.

bikes.year_notes: NEW MODEL, verified: launched December 2025 (Electrek 'Talaria Komodo launched as new 65 MPH electric dirt bike', Dec 31 2025; New Atlas; GritShift pre-ride check; on Talaria US/Canada/UK dealer pages). Verified specs: 32kW peak, 754Nm, 105km/h (65mph), 0-60 in ~4.5s, 96V 45Ah pack (4.3kWh), up to 115km range at 45km/h, full-size 21in front / 18in rear wheels with CST or Maxxis rubber, dual-chamber air fork with 250mm travel, rear air shock with 90mm stroke, four modes (Eco/Sport/Hyper/Reverse), 4-level regen, 98kg (216lb), $5,699 US. This is Talaria's 450-class enduro answer to the Storm Bee/Stark segment. Brand-new: zero takeoff resale evidence, so everything except the full-size tires prices at floors. Nothing pools with any other Talaria - unique 96V platform.
bikes.blurb: Talaria's new full-size 96V enduro, launched December 2025. Real dirt bike dimensions mean its tires and controls have crossover demand, but the parts market itself does not exist yet.

stock_parts.comp_note: Dual-chamber air fork, 250mm travel (New Atlas/dealer spec pages); supplier not published. Full-size fork on a 216lb bike - swap pressure unknown this early. No resale market; floor value only.

stock_parts.catalog_name: Talaria Komodo stock rear air shock (2026+)
stock_parts.comp_note: Rear air shock, 90mm stroke (New Atlas). No resale market; floor value only.
Unique 96V platform - pools with nothing else in the catalog. No aftermarket support or resale market yet; floor value only.

4.3kWh 96V pack (Electrek/New Atlas). Highest-energy Talaria pack ever, but months old with zero resale history and no replacement-price anchor - floor value until real solds exist. Revisit fast; this will not stay a floor part.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock 21in front / 18in rear knobby pair (CST or Maxxis)
stock_parts.comp_note: Full-size 21/18 moto sizes (New Atlas: CST or Maxxis depending on run - record the brand at intake). Broad dirtbike buyer pool exists for the size, same dynamics as the Storm Bee tire row. No Komodo-specific comps; pegged low.

upgrade.name: Shinko 546 front and 505 rear tire set
upgrade.note: Terrain-specific rubber for a full-size electric enduro.

stock_parts.comp_note: No used market exists. Floor value only.

bikes.battery: 5.2kWh lithium pack (nominal voltage not yet verified)
bikes.year_notes: NEW MODEL, verified with caveats: launched as a 2025 model and fully available through US dealers in 2026 (talaria.us.com, talariausa.us.com, talariaebikeshop.com; Talaria USA lists it in the current lineup). Specs from dealer/press pages: 10kW nominal / 28kW peak mid-drive, 630Nm, ~70mph, 0-60 under 5s, 5.2kWh pack (up to 93mi claimed), three modes (Eco/Sport/Hyper), 4.3in TOUCHSCREEN display with Bluetooth/voice/music, 35in seat height, 220lb, $6,949. CAUTION: pack nominal VOLTAGE is not consistently published across sources - record the label at intake before cataloging the battery. Positioned above the Komodo as the flagship. Zero takeoff resale evidence; floors everywhere except where a real buyer pool exists.
bikes.blurb: Talaria's flagship, a 28kW halo bike sold alongside the Komodo. Too new for a parts market - catalog carefully, price at floors, and verify the battery label because published specs disagree.

stock_parts.comp_note: Fork type/supplier not reliably published - record markings and travel at intake. No resale market; floor value only.
Shock spec not reliably published. No resale market; floor value only.
Flagship-power controller with Eco/Sport/Hyper modes. Platform voltage unverified - do not pool with Komodo 96V parts without matching labels. No resale market; floor value only.

5.2kWh is the consistent published figure; nominal voltage is NOT consistent across sources, so verify the pack label before listing. Largest pack of any Talaria. Zero resale history; floor value until solds exist. Revisit fast.

4.3in touchscreen with Bluetooth/smartphone/voice features per dealer pages - the most complex dash in the Talaria line, so crash-replacement demand will exist. No resale market yet; floor value only.
Full-size fitment per the 35in-seat-height chassis; exact sizes not consistently published - record at intake. Priced off the generic full-size knobby pool, low.
No used market exists. Floor value only.

bikes.motor: Electric dirt bike, three selectable power modes (150/200/300cc-equivalent); full specs unpublished pre-launch
bikes.year_notes: NEW MODEL, verified as announced but PRE-RELEASE: unveiled at CES 2026 (Segway press release via PR Newswire; Cycle News; Powersports Business), $5,299.99 MSRP, availability spring-summer 2026. It descends from the X1000 prototype that ran the 2025 Dakar Future Mission 1000 class and effectively replaces the discontinued X260/X160 dirt eBike line. Published so far: three power modes (150/200/300cc equivalents), claimed best-in-class 24.7% power-to-weight ratio, virtual electronic clutch, selectable traction control, app-adjustable wheelie angle, parental controls/geo-fencing, 2yr battery/motor/frame warranty. Motor kW, battery V/Ah, suspension, brakes, and wheel sizes are NOT published - Segway says full specs come closer to launch. Every part row here is a placeholder pool at floor value: verify all component specs at first intake and re-dossier this bike once production units ship. Do NOT pool anything with X260/X160 parts.
bikes.blurb: Segway's CES 2026 return to dirt bikes, replacing the X260 era. Ships mid-2026 - this entry exists so the first takeoffs have somewhere to land, not because a parts market exists.

stock_parts.comp_note: Component specs unpublished pre-launch. Record brand/travel at first intake. Floor value only.
Component specs unpublished pre-launch. Floor value only.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock controller (3 power modes, virtual e-clutch, traction control)
stock_parts.comp_note: Software-heavy unit (e-clutch, TCS, app integration per Segway CES material) - likely paired/locked to the bike like the X260's app pairing; note pairing status in any listing. Floor value only.
Battery spec unpublished; 2-year battery warranty confirmed. Record V/Ah label at first intake. Floor value only until the platform and resale market exist.
Sizes unpublished. If they land at standard moto sizes there will be a crossover buyer pool; re-price then. Floor value only.
No market exists. Floor value only.

bikes.year_notes: NEW MODEL, verified: Super73's all-new A-Series launched March 19 2026 (Super73 press release via PR Newswire; Electrek; Powersports Business) - the brand's first full lineup overhaul, replacing the legacy Z/S/R platforms in the range (legacy models' fate not formally announced; treat legacy entries as ended at 2025 until stated otherwise). MZFT is the 27in-seat-height compact ('The Backyard Brawler' per super73.com), $1,995 base / $2,395 SE. Verified A-Series core spec (super73.com compare page, Electrek, EVSHIFT): 500W nominal motor, 20mph, GEN2 modular 52V 520Wh packs at 8lb each with dual-pack capability (~1kWh total), 2-piston brakes, rigid fork on core models, 4.5in-wide tires (diameters 16-20in vary by model - record at intake). SE trim adds 780Wh battery, 750W motor, adjustable front suspension, better brakes, and a new digital display. Zero takeoff market this young; floors everywhere except tires.
bikes.blurb: The smallest of Super73's new-for-2026 A-Series. Commuter culture, not teardown culture - expect light takeoff flow led by tire and battery spares.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared GEN2 pack across the A-Series (one pool for all three models). Modular dual-pack design means owners ADD packs rather than pull them, so takeoffs are crash/warranty stock only. No resale history; floor value until solds exist.
SE-trim larger pack (super73.com SE product pages), shared across A-Series SE models - one pool. No resale history; floor value.

4.5in-wide rubber per the A-Series compare page; wheel DIAMETER varies across the A-Series (16-20in), so this pool is model-specific until diameters are confirmed at intake. Super73 tire-swap culture is the strongest takeoff generator on the brand; pegged off the legacy BDGR pool, low.

stock_parts.stock_part: SE adjustable front suspension fork (core trim is rigid)
stock_parts.comp_note: Core A-Series models run a rigid fork (super73.com compare data); the SE's adjustable suspension fork is the listable takeoff. Verify which trim the donor is. No resale market; floor value.
Super73 custom-seat culture should carry over to the A-Series. No resale market yet; floor value.
Shared A-Series cockpit pool until proven otherwise. Low-value part; floor.

stock_parts.stock_part: Stock A-Series display and throttle (SE: new digital display)
stock_parts.comp_note: New-generation electronics - NOT interchangeable with legacy Super73 display pools. SE trims carry the upgraded digital display; note trim in the listing. Spare-part demand only; floor value.

bikes.year_notes: NEW MODEL, verified: A-Series launch March 19 2026 (Super73 press release; Electrek). M1D is the 30in-seat-height mid-size ('The Sweet Spot' / 'The Crowd-Pleaser' per super73.com), $2,495 base / $2,895 SE. Core spec shared with the A-Series (see MZFT notes): 500W nominal, 20mph, GEN2 52V 520Wh modular packs (dual capable), 2-piston brakes, rigid fork core / adjustable suspension SE, 4.5in tires. Range: single 520Wh ~20mi, dual ~40mi; SE single 780Wh 25-30mi, dual 50-55mi (super73.com compare page). Zero takeoff market; floors except tires.
bikes.blurb: The mid-size of the 2026 A-Series and the likely volume seller. Same commuter parts story as its siblings - thin takeoff flow until the install base grows.

stock_parts.comp_note: Shared GEN2 pool with all A-Series models. Floor value; no resale history.

bikes.year_notes: NEW MODEL, verified: A-Series launch March 19 2026 (Super73 press release; Electrek). B1G is the 33in-seat-height large platform ('The Rogue Riser' / SE 'The Final Boss' per super73.com), built for taller riders - the first Super73 platform sized this way. $2,595 base / $2,995 SE. Core spec shared with the A-Series (see MZFT notes): 500W nominal, 20mph, GEN2 52V 520Wh modular packs (dual capable), 2-piston brakes, rigid fork core / adjustable suspension SE, 4.5in tires with the largest diameters in the series. Zero takeoff market; floors except tires.
bikes.blurb: The tall-rider platform of the 2026 A-Series. Largest wheels in the lineup; otherwise the same commuter parts economics as its siblings.

bikes.motor: 5.4kW rear hub motor (72V era); 80V hub motor, 18kW max, on 2024+ RCR 80V
bikes.year_notes: Three battery eras, verified: launch pack was 72V 23Ah (Panasonic NCR18650PF, 20S8P - johnangel.nyc battery specifications); the 41Ah pack (200x 21700 cells, 20S10P) was announced Nov 16 2020 (Electrek 'ONYX RCR gets major updates and doubles range') and shipped on 2021+ orders, backwards compatible; the RCR 80V (45Ah, new controller with Thrill/Cruise modes and revised throttle mapping) launched 2024 (onyxmotors.com RCR 80V page; johnangel.nyc 80V explainer). Frame battery-tray width grew across revisions v1.0 to v1.2 to v1.5 (johnangel.nyc), so pack/tray fit is revision-dependent - record the frame version at intake. Company timeline: founder died 2021, brand relaunched with the limited RCR LTD in 2023 (thepack.news), then the 80V. Stock Kelly controller spans all 72V versions (v1.0 through v1.75 and LTD, per johnangel.nyc Sicko Mode notes); the 80V controller is a distinct part. 2026 UPDATE: the RCR 80V is confirmed current and actively sold through mid-2026 - Forbes reviewed it June 30 2026, ONYX showcased street-ready 80V customs at the One Motorcycle Show April 2026 (Business Wire), and onyxmotors.com sells multiple configurations from $5,199 (dual-sport and street trims). No verified component changes for 2026, so the 80V rows extend to 2024-2026 with catalog names unchanged. No evidence of suspension/tire/seat spec changes across years.
bikes.blurb: Cafe-racer moped with real motorcycle speed. Loyal owner base mods steadily but the parts market is a fraction of the Sur-Ron world, so resale is slow and thin.

stock_parts.catalog_name: ONYX RCR stock 350mm rear shock pair (takeoff)
stock_parts.comp_note: No evidence of a shock spec change across revisions. ONYX sells upgraded rear shocks direct, so stock pairs come off. No sold comps found; pegged low for a small owner base.

upgrade.name: ONYX Motors upgraded RCR rear shocks
upgrade.note: Factory upgrade path. Stock shocks are budget units on a fast bike.

stock_parts.comp_note: Fork swaps less common than rear shocks. No comps found; conservative estimate. No year-variant evidence.
Dual sport tire swaps are the standard RCR mod, and the 2026 80V ships in dual-sport and street (slick) trims - note which tread the donor carried. Used street takeoffs cheap; no sold comps found.

upgrade.name: Shinko 244 dual sport tire pair
upgrade.note: The RCR community default for mixed surface riding.

stock_parts.comp_note: Cafe seat swaps happen for style. Small market, occasional demand from crash repairs; low estimate.

The Kelly controller spans every 72V RCR revision (v1.0-v1.75 incl. LTD, per johnangel.nyc); community 'Sicko Mode' tuning runs on it. The 2024+ RCR 80V uses a different controller - do not pool. No resale sales found; floor value only.

Distinct 80V unit with new firmware (Thrill and Cruise modes, revised throttle mapping, display temperature readout - johnangel.nyc 80V explainer). Platform confirmed current through mid-2026 (Forbes June 2026), so the pool extends to 2024-2026; catalog name kept for DB merge. Too new and too rare for any resale evidence; floor value only.

Launch-era pack (johnangel.nyc battery specifications). These 18650 packs are now 5+ years old with heavy cycle wear typical, and many were replaced by the backwards-compatible 41Ah upgrade, so surviving takeoffs price near salvage. No sold comps found; pegged very conservative.

The doubled-range pack announced Nov 2020 (Electrek), standard on 2021+ 72V bikes including the 2023 LTD; catalog_name kept from v1 since the 41Ah spec already identifies it. Big pack with real replacement cost, tiny buyer pool, unverifiable health. Frame tray width varies by revision (v1.0/v1.2/v1.5) - confirm donor frame version. No sold comps found; pegged very conservative for the pack class.

Pack for the 2024+ RCR 80V (onyxmotors.com). NOT electrically interchangeable with 72V bikes. Platform current through mid-2026, so the pool extends to 2024-2026; catalog name kept for DB merge. Newest and highest-value pack in the family but effectively zero resale history; pegged conservative at 40 percent of a cautious comp.
